source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Pacific Sharav Synthetic Oil Based Mud Discharge

event.top_event: Unintentional discharge of Synthetic Oil Based Mud into Gulf of Mexico
actions_taken: Driller shut down mud pumps and picked up off bottom
MSO shut down transfer of water overboard and closed all valves not associated with drilling alignment
Assistant Driller investigated mud system issue
Modified semi-weekly discharge procedure to show proper valve alignment
Implemented requirement for competent individual to verify valve alignment prior to opening overboard valves
Incident discussed with all crew members in Weekly Safety Meeting
Investigation findings shared with entire fleet
event.summary: On June 27, 2017 at 2120 hours, the Pacific Sharav discharged 29 barrels of 14.0 PPG Synthetic Oil Based Mud into the Gulf of Mexico. The discharge occurred due to misalignment of two valves on the low pressure header while the Mud System Operator was pumping deck drain water from the muddy water tank overboard. The valves were incorrectly opened, allowing mud from the active mud system to enter the overboard line. The incident was caused by human error and failure to follow approved procedures, specifically the absence of the Mud Engineer who is responsible for verifying valve alignment during the semi-weekly discharge procedure.
recommendations: Modify semi-weekly discharge procedure to explicitly show proper valve alignment
Require verification of valve alignment by a competent individual prior to opening overboard valves
Require competent individual to verify proper valve alignment when more than one type of fluid is in the active mud system
Conduct crew training on incident findings

hazards.name: Synthetic Oil Based Mud in active mud system
Pressurized mud system during drilling operations
Overboard discharge system for deck drain water

threats.name: Valve misalignment during overboard discharge operations
Absence of Mud Engineer supervision during fluid transfer
Inadequate procedure clarity on valve alignment verification

consequences.name: Discharge of 29 barrels of Synthetic Oil Based Mud into Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Mud Engineer verification of valve alignment
controls.barrier_role: Prevent incorrect valve alignment during overboard discharge
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to ensure correct valve configuration before discharge operations

supporting_text: The Mud Engineer is also tasked with verifying valve alignments to avoid fluid contamination and accidental discharges of hazardous fluids into the marine environment.
Without the Mud Engineer to assist in the procedure and verify valve alignment, two valves on the low pressure header were incorrectly opened while pumping water overboard

controls.name: Semi-weekly discharge procedure for muddy water tank
controls.barrier_role: Provide documented procedure to ensure safe discharge operations
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to guide correct execution of discharge operations

supporting_text: the MSO then returned to his task of sheen testing the water and pumping it overboard in accordance with Pacific Drilling's semi-weekly discharge of the muddy water tank procedure
Failure to follow the approved procedure. The Mud Engineer was not present during the fluid transfer and valve alignment.

controls.name: Driller monitoring of standpipe pressure and mud pit levels
controls.barrier_role: Detect anomalies in mud system operation
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection control to identify system deviations

supporting_text: While drilling out cement, the Driller noticed a decrease in standpipe pressure.
Soon after talking to the MSO, the Driller noticed a fluid decrease in the active mud pit and called the MSO to have him check the valve alignment.

controls.name: Immediate shutdown and investigation response
controls.barrier_role: Stop discharge and contain the incident
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to halt ongoing discharge

supporting_text: The Driller picked up off bottom to stop drilling, shut down the mud pumps, and sent the Assistant Driller (AD) to investigate the mud system issue.
The MSO shut down the transfer of water overboard and closed all valves not associated with the drilling alignment.

controls.name: Competent individual verification of valve alignment (post-incident modification)
controls.barrier_role: Ensure correct valve configuration before overboard discharge
controls.lod_basis: Enhanced procedural control requiring competency verification

supporting_text: valve alignment will have to be verified by an individual deemed competent by Pacific Drilling prior to overboard valves being opened
This competent individual will also have to verify proper valve alignment when there is more than one type of fluid in the active mud system.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
